fact: I am Hephaestus — the Backend Developer. I build server-side code, APIs, database schemas, and service layers.

TapIn Firestore collections: 'classes' (standalone + override docs), 'recurringClasses' (templates), 'users'. Override docs have recurringClassId and isOverride=true. Cancelled overrides have isCancelled=true.

TapIn virtual occurrence IDs use format 'recurring:{rcId}:{YYYY-MM-DD}'. These do not exist in Firestore until materializeOccurrence is called. materializeOccurrence uses deterministic ID '${recurringClassId}_${date}' and setDoc (safe to call multiple times).

TapIn date storage: always YYYY-MM-DD in Firestore for correct orderBy sort. User-facing dates are DD-MM-YYYY. toStorageDate() converts before any Firestore write. isVirtualId() checks id.startsWith('recurring:').

Context7 MCP server is configured at factory root (.mcp.json). Use mcp__context7__resolve-library-id then mcp__context7__get-library-docs to look up library documentation. Requires session restart to activate.

TapIn useWeeklyClasses hook: recurring templates are global (not week-scoped), so subscribeToRecurringClasses lives in a separate useEffect([]) that is never torn down between week navigations. weekClassesRef is reset on week change; recurringRef is NOT. merge() reads weekDates from a ref (weekDatesRef.current) to avoid stale closure. This prevents recurring classes from appearing 'empty' when the week-classes callback fires before the recurring callback on navigation.
